text: If you're planning to be outside much today, keep an eye on that hazy sky, the wildfire smoke drifting down from Canada has bumped our air quality to moderate, and it'll hang around into the afternoon before storms clear it out some. Otherwise it's a mostly sunny day with a high near 82, but the models are arguing about how wet it gets: our forecasters and the American model like a lower rain chance, while the European model's much wetter, so I'd keep the rain jacket handy for those afternoon and evening thunderstorms. On the lake, southwest wind builds into a stiff 15 to 25 knots out of the northwest tonight, so get your boating done well before evening. Tonight clears out and turns comfortably cool, setting up a genuinely gorgeous Friday, sunny, high near 81, with none of today's smoke or storm worries. Good window on the water or trails today before roughly 3pm; after that, watch the sky and don't push your luck out on the broad lake.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-16T11:00:00+00:00): Cloudy, 66F feels 66F, humidity 73%, wind SSW 7 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: Areas of smoke before 1pm, then patchy smoke and a slight chance of rain showers between 1pm and 2pm, then patchy smoke and showers and thunderstorms likely. Mostly sunny, with a high near 82. West wind 5 to 16 mph, with gusts as high as 31 mph. Chance of precipitation is 60%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
  Tonight: Mostly clear, with a low around 59. Northwest wind 7 to 14 mph, with gusts as high as 26 mph. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
  Friday: Sunny, with a high near 81. Northwest wind around 8 mph.
  Friday Night: Partly cloudy, with a low around 60. Southwest wind 2 to 7 mph.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-16T11:13:00+00:00):
  1. Wildfire smoke will continue to degrade the air quality. An Air Quality Alert is in effect for northern New York and southern Vermont today.
  2. Thunderstorm chances today and over the weekend.
  3. Seasonably warm temperatures and a brief dry period are expected early next week, then another round of showers and potential storms towards midweek.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 243 AM EDT Thursday... No significant changes.
LAKE (broad waters):
  Today: Southwest winds 10 to 15 knots, becoming northwest and increasing to 15 to 25 knots. Partly sunny. Areas of smoke in the morning, then patchy smoke in the afternoon. A chance of showers with a slight chance of thunderstorms in the afternoon. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Tonight: Northwest winds 15 to 20 knots, diminishing to 10 to 15 knots. Clear. Waves 1 to 3 feet.
  Friday: Northwest winds 5 to 15 knots. Sunny. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Friday Night: Northwest winds 10 knots or less, becoming south. Partly cloudy. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 75F, level 96.24 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-16: highs [GFS 80, Euro 80, ICON 77] spread 3F; precip chance [GFS 24%, Euro 76%, ICON 30%]
  2026-07-17: highs [GFS 79, Euro 75, ICON 76] spread 4F; precip chance [GFS 0%, Euro 2%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-18: highs [GFS 83, Euro 79, ICON 68] spread 15F; precip chance [GFS 92%, Euro 87%, ICON 93%]
AIR: AQI 60 Moderate (PM2.5) — On Thursday, July 16th, smoke from Canadian wildfires is expected to impact Vermont.  Southern Vermont is expected to be Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups and an air quality alert is in place for that area.  Moderate particulate matter from smoke may be present in the remainder of Vermont.
SUN: rise 2026-07-16T05:23-04:00, set 2026-07-16T20:34-04:00, UV max 6.9
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 80, low 59: Showers and thunderstorms late. Highs in the low 80s and lows in the upper 50s.
  high 79, low 60: A few clouds. Highs in the upper 70s and lows in the low 60s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: A couple lingering showers are possible early Sunday, but after that the sun will emerge leaving us with a nice, but cool afternoon with temperatures in the low to mid-70s. We’ll warm back up to where we should be in the low 80s early next week with sunshine, Monday, but then more rain is possible Tuesday.
NBC5 FIRST WARNING (2026-07-16T10:14:00Z): Smoky sky lingers, with isolated PM storms Thursday — Sunny and pleasant Friday, few showers and storms this weekend
